sources.notes: Easy version asks to permute [l,r] with l=0 and maximize sum of bitwise OR with the original order.
Official hints use the upper bound x|y <= x+y and pair values with complements 2^k-1-x.

annotation.summary: Reorder the array [0,n] to maximize the sum of a_i | b_i and output one optimal permutation.
annotation.constraints: The easy version has l=0, so the interval is a prefix [0,n] and can be paired greedily from the largest value.
annotation.core_idea: For any pair x,y, x|y <= x+y, so the total sum is at most the sum of all elements in both arrays. Equality holds exactly when x&y=0 for every pair. For a full range from 0, pairing x with the available complement (2^k-1)-x for the smallest all-ones mask covering x creates pairs with disjoint bits, achieving the upper bound.
annotation.complexity: O(n log n) or O(n) with a simple available-set implementation.
tricks: OR upper bound by pair sum

solution_variants.name: all-ones-complement-greedy
solution_variants.summary: Process values from high to low and pair each unpaired value with its complement under the current all-ones mask.

tags.evidence: The construction is based on disjoint binary bits and complements under masks of the form 2^k-1.
The task requires outputting an explicit optimal permutation, built by complement pairing.
Processing from the largest remaining value chooses the only complement that can keep x&y=0 at the current mask level.
